answer:
\boxed{A}

The study found a statistically significant association (P = .03) between the absence of the Hawkins sign and the development of avascular necrosis, suggesting it can predict necrosis. None of the cases with a positive Hawkins sign developed necrosis, while 4 out of 11 cases with a negative sign did. Thus, the Hawkins sign appears to be a useful predictor.
